mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the realtime web. It is ideal for applications requiring flexible data models and real-time capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

```

> This is the official repository for SurrealDB Helm charts.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace and version.

```bash

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --create-namespace --version 3.0.2

```

> Ensure the namespace does not already exist.

steps.title: Create a Custom Resource for SurrealDB
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) for SurrealDB to define its configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  replicas: 3
  storage:
    size: 10Gi
EOF

```

> Adjust replicas and storage size as needed.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  replicas: 3
  storage:
    size: 10Gi
EOF

steps.title: Verify SurrealDB is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the SurrealDB pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n surrealdb

```

> Look for 'Running' status in the output.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes SurrealDB and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surrealdb

kubectl delete namespace surrealdb

```

> This command removes the Helm release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade SurrealDB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp surrealdb-0:/data /backup/surrealdb-data

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --version 3.0.2

kubectl get pods -n surrealdb

```

> Backup your data before proceeding.
> This command upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb' and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The persistent volume claim may not have enough storage allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the storage size in the Custom Resource and reapply it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n surrealdb' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly defined and check the service endpoints.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new query optimizations and improved data replication features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed deprecated API endpoints for data access.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your data schema as per the new guidelines.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb' showing 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
